raw_gpt: {"page":133,"text_mcq":[{"question":"For a slotted terminal solder connection, what should the solder form with the portion of the lead or wire that contacts the terminal?","options":{"A":"A fillet with the contacted portion of the lead or wire","B":"A ball completely covering the terminal top","C":"A coating only on the outside of the terminal","D":"A bridge to adjacent hardware"},"correct_option":"A","answer":"The standard states that solder should form a fillet with the portion of the lead or wire in contact with the terminal.","topic":"solder_joint","evidence":{"block_ids":["p0133-text-02"],"quote":"Solder should form a fillet with that portion of the lead or wire that is in contact with the terminal."},"confidence":"high"},{"question":"According to the slotted-terminal solder criteria, which condition should be avoided even though solder may completely fill the slot?","options":{"A":"The solder being built up on top of the terminal","B":"The lead or wire remaining discernible in the terminal","C":"The solder forming a fillet where the wire contacts the terminal","D":"The slot being completely filled with solder"},"correct_option":"A","answer":"Solder may fill the slot, but it should not be built up on top of the terminal.","topic":"solder_joint","evidence":{"block_ids":["p0133-text-02"],"quote":"Solder may completely fill the slot but should not be built up on top of the terminal."},"confidence":"high"}],"image
